title: Devices and method for carrying out cross-border electronic document workflow
abstract: Provided are devices and a method for carrying out cross-border electronic document workflow by performing the following operations: receiving an electronic document signed with an electronic signature, identifying a sender and a recipient of the electronic document while determining an encryption standard used by them based on an electronic signature certificate correspondent to the electronic signature, identifying an electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender and an electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient, if the identified sender and recipient use different encryption standards, sending a request to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender to verify the validity of the electronic signature, additional signing of the received electronic document with an electronic signature which data is received and which corresponds to the encryption standard used by the recipient, and sending the twice-signed electronic document to the server of the electronic document workflow operator.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A computing device for carrying out cross-border electronic document workflow, the device comprising a processor configured to obtain electronic signatures to allow signing an electronic document and further configured to perform at least the following operations: receiving at least one electronic document signed with an electronic signature, identifying a sender and a recipient of said electronic document while ensuring the determination of the encryption standard used by them based on an electronic signature certificate correspondent to said electronic signature, identifying the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender and the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient based on said electronic signature certificate, if the identified sender and recipient use different encryption standards, sending a request to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender to verify the validity of the senderâs said electronic signature using a certificate chain of trust, additional signing of said received electronic document with an electronic signature which data is received by said processor and which corresponds to the encryption standard used by the recipient when receiving the confirmation of the validity of the senderâs electronic signature from said server of the electronic document workflow operator, and sending said twice-signed electronic document to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient. 2. A computing device for carrying out cross-border electronic document workflow, the device comprising: a communication module configured to receive at least one electronic document signed with an electronic signature, an identifying module configured to connect to the communication module to receive said electronic document therefrom and to perform at least the following operations: - identifying a sender and a recipient of said electronic document ensuring the determination of the encryption standard used by them based on an electronic signature certificate correspondent to said electronic signature, - identifying the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender and the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to said identified recipient based on said electronic signature certificate, if the identified sender and recipient use different encryption standards, a validating module configured to connect to the identifying module to receive said identification results therefrom and configured to send a request to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender to verify the validity of said electronic signature of the sender using a certificate chain of trust, a signing module configured to connect to the validating module to obtain said validation result therefrom and further configured to sign said received document with one more electronic document correspondent to the encryption standard used by the recipient under the condition of validity of the senderâs specified electronic signature, wherein the signing module is further configured to connect to the communication module ensuring that said twice-signed electronic document is forwarded to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient. 3. A computing device for carrying out cross-border electronic document workflow, the device comprising: a communication module configured to receive at least one electronic document signed with an electronic signature, and 

 a signing module configured to connect to the communication module to receive said electronic document therefrom and configured to perform at least the following operations: identifying a sender and a recipient of said electronic document ensuring the determination of the encryption standard used by them based on the electronic signature certificate correspondent to said electronic signature, identifying the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender and the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient based on said electronic signature certificate, if the identified sender and recipient use different encryption standards, sending a request to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender to verify the validity of the senderâs specified electronic signature using a certificate chain of trust, additional signing of said received electronic document with one more electronic signature correspondent to the encryption standard used by the recipient when receiving the confirmation of the validity of the sender's electronic signature from said server of the electronic document workflow operator, and connecting to the communication module to allow sending said twice-signed electronic document to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient. 4. A method for carrying out cross-border electronic document workflow performed on a computing device, said method comprising: receiving at least one electronic document signed with an electronic signature, identifying a sender and a recipient of said electronic document while ensuring the determination of the encryption standard used by them based on an electronic signature certificate correspondent to said electronic signature, identifying the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender and the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient based on said electronic signature certificate, if the identified sender and recipient use different encryption standards, sending a request to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified sender to verify the validity of the senderâs said electronic signature using a certificate chain of trust, upon receiving the validity confirmation related to the senderâs electronic signature from said server of the electronic document workflow operator, receiving data about one more electronic signature correspondent to the encryption standard used by the recipient ensuring the additional signing of said received electronic document with said one more electronic signature, and sending said twice-signed electronic document to the server of the electronic document workflow operator correspondent to the identified recipient.
